'''Noori''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Noori sector/Legends|Noori sector's]] [[Noori system/Legends|Noori system]] within [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] southeast quadrant, located in an area of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] overlapping with a part of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] began [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] the region during the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]], resulting in widespread colonization in the following [[Time/Legends|millennia]]. Noori was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Noorian]]s, a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with striped [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. [[Tahl]], a Noorian [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived during the last decades of the Republic, hailed from the planet.

==Description==
Noori was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="DOTD" /> in the [[Noori system/Legends|Noori system]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Noori sector/Legends|Noori sector]],<ref name="Atlas Appendix" /> located within the southeast quadrant of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TEA" /> The area overlapped with [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]],<ref name="Slice" /> a wedge of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] between the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] of [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] and [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]].<ref name="TEA" /> The planet was capable of supporting life.<ref name="DOTD" />

==History==
[[File:NoorianTahl.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Tahl, a member of the Noorian species]]
The part of the galaxy that contained Noori<ref name="Slice" /> was charted by [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] of the expanding [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]]. Beginning in [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]], the period marked the start of the Slice's extensive colonization. By the last [[Time/Legends|millennium]]'' of the Republic, virtually all of the inhabitable planets in the Slice had been populated for thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Toward the end of the [[New Sith Wars]], between [[1004 BBY]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the surroundings of Noori were unaligned space, with neither the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] nor the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the Republic having a firm hold of the area. By [[137 ABY]], Noori was within the territory of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Inhabitants==
Noori was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Noorian]]s,<ref name="Noorian" /> a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who were distinguishable from [[Human/Legends|Humans]] by their striped [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tahl]],<ref name="JDS3" /> born in [[92 BBY/Legends|92 BBY]],<ref name="92 BBY">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope]]'' establishes Tahl to be the same ages as [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], whose birth year has been confirmed to be 92 BBY e.g. in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], p. xx {{C|Time Line}}.</ref> was a native of the planet, though [[Gender/Legends|she]] left it early in life to be [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] of the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="DOTD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Noori was first mentioned in [[Jedi Apprentice: The Defenders of the Dead|''The Defenders of the Dead'']], the fifth installment in the [[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice|''Jedi Apprentice'']] series. Published in [[1999]], the young reader novel was written by [[Jude Watson]] and contains the only [[in-universe]] reference to the planet thus far.<ref name="DOTD" /> Noori has since been mentioned in the "Tahl" entry of the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|212|Tahl, Master}}</ref>

[[File:O-10.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Galactic grid location of O-10]]
In [[2009]], the Noori [[Star system/Legends|system]] was included in the appendix of ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', a third-generation [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|Essential Guide]] by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] that focuses on the [[astrography]] of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe.<ref name="TEA" /> Though the planet Noori is not placed within the eponymous system in either ''The Defenders of the Dead''<ref name="DOTD" /> or ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'',<ref name="CSWE" /> Fry has stated that "planets not assigned a system in [a] published source can be assumed to reside in [the] system of [the] same name."<ref name="Fry">{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/294887358405410816|[[Jason Fry]]|archivedate=20130704140111}}</ref> ''The Essential Atlas'' placed the Noori system in the Expansion Region at the grid coordinates O-10.<ref name="TEA" /> The same information was included in the Online Appendix of the [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|''Atlas'' Online Companion]] at [[StarWars.com]]. In a September [[2012]] update to the Online Appendix, the Noori system was also given a sector placement within the Noori sector.<ref name="Atlas Appendix" />
